behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text. MOCK-MISS is low if we feed real copied snippets as fixtures; no OS/network/subprocess dependence (REAL-INFRA no), and no LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the lint function with table-driven cases: clean template passes; template containing verbatim/near-verbatim phase-instruction lines from SKILL.md fails with precise violation output.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Leakage can be introduced during interpolation/fixture merge, which a pure template-unit test can miss (MOCK-MISS yes). Requires wiring real prompt builder modules together; no external network or LLM required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly path with production-like fixtures/interpolation and assert the final prompt text does not contain banned phase-instruction fragments/patterns derived from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is a source-of-truth wiring check across loader + invocation + prompt construction, typically via local filesystem skill files. Pure mocks can hide hard-coded fallback behavior (MOCK-HIDE yes). No real external API required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real test invocation against actual plugin/skill files on disk; assert the loaded context appears in the invocation inputs and that no hard-coded phase-rule block is present in the static prompt template path.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Core correctness claim depends on model behavior changing when skill context quality changes (LLM-DEP yes). A deterministic/mock model can falsely pass and hide true regression sensitivity (MOCK-HIDE yes). Single real model step, not multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B behavioral smoke test with identical user-style prompt: (A) intact SKILL.md context, (B) degraded/removed target section. Using the real model path, assert pass on A and fail on B under fixed harness settings, recording outcome deltas.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Need to verify execution order across model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser modules plus local filesystem artifact creation. Unit tests can miss ordering bugs introduced by orchestration glue (MOCK-MISS yes).
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real response-processing pipeline with malformed/non-JSON model text; assert a raw-output artifact is written first and remains available even when downstream parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is assertion semantics itself. Can be caught with crafted outputs where keywords exist but structure is wrong; no real infra or LLM required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with adversarial fixtures: prompt-echo text containing expected keywords but invalid schema/field placement must fail, while correctly structured output passes.
